title: Relationship Between a Relation, Its Zero-One Matrix, and Its Directed Graph
description: A visual representation showing how a relation on a finite set can be equivalently represented by a zero-one matrix and a directed graph, and how a computer can translate between these representations.
relationships: A relation R on a finite set A defines the ordered pairs. The zero-one matrix M_R is built from R, where M_R[i,j]=1 if (a_i, a_j) is in R. The directed graph G_R is built from R, with vertices for elements of A and edges for ordered pairs. A computer algorithm can generate any one representation from any other.
